name: pagetable_is_empty_ignores_kernel_l4_entries

proof fn phi_3_pagetable_is_empty_ignores_kernel_l4_entries(pt: PageTable)
    requires

name: resolve_l3_skips_ps_check
code:
proof fn phi_5_resolve_l3_skips_ps_check(pt: PageTable, l4i: L4Index, l3i: L3Index)
    requires
        pt.kernel_l4_end <= l4i < 512,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).is_Some(),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr),
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].is_init(),
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].value()[l3i].perm.present,
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].value()[l3i].perm.ps,
    ensures
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l3(l4i, l3i).is_Some(),
{
}
reason: spec_resolve_mapping_l3 returns Some even when the L3 entry has ps=true (indicating a 1G huge page) — the resolution continues to L3 level without checking whether the entry is a leaf, allowing the same entry to be interpreted as both a 1G mapping and a pointer to an L2 table
